title: Minimum Brackets To Remove
prompt:
Given a string s containing lowercase letters and parentheses '(' and ')', remove the minimum number of parentheses so that the resulting string is valid.

A string is valid if:
- It is empty, or
- All parentheses are closed and properly nested.

Return any valid string with the minimum number of parentheses removed.
constraintQuestions: Does the string contain only lowercase letters and parentheses? (Assume yes.)
Is an empty string considered valid? (Yes.)
Can we return any valid answer if multiple are possible? (Yes.)
Do we only remove parentheses (not letters)?
Should we aim for O(n) time?

walkthrough.title: Stack + array approach
walkthrough.body:
Convert the string to an array so we can mark removals.
Use a stack of indices for '('.
Traverse left to right:
- If you see '(', push its index.
- If you see ')' and the stack is empty, mark it for removal.
- If you see ')' and the stack has items, pop to match it.
After the scan, any leftover indices in the stack are unmatched '(' so mark them for removal.
Finally, join the array back into a string.

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(n) for the scan and final join.
Space: O(n) for the array and stack.
